title: MailerLite vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite vs Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and ratings to find the best email marketing tool for your business.
h1: MailerLite vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: MailerLite and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they target different business types and budgets. MailerLite emphasizes affordability and ease of use with a free tier, while Constant Contact focuses on comprehensive features for local businesses and nonprofits. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right platform for your marketing needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, social media ads

choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer with a tight budget and want to start for free
You need landing pages, pop-ups, and surveys integrated into your email platform
You want strong automation features without committing to paid plans immediately
choose_b_if: You need SMS marketing and social media advertising alongside email
You're organizing events and need event management functionality
You prefer a platform specifically designed for local businesses and nonprofits

faq.q: Is MailerLite better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: MailerLite scores higher on user reviews and offers a free tier, making it better for budget-conscious businesses. However, Constant Contact has more advanced features like SMS and event management. The best choice depends on your specific needs and budget.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Constant Contact?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, with a free plan supporting up to 1,000 subscribers and paid plans starting at $9/month. Constant Contact has no free tier and starts at $12/month, making MailerLite the more affordable option overall.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support list imports and exports, making migration straightforward. You can typically export your contacts and campaign history from Constant Contact and import them into MailerLite. Most integrations and automations will need to be reconfigured on the new platform.
